summary:
Efgartigimod reduces pathogenic IgG antibodies by inhibiting the FcRn receptor, offering a targeted mechanism to treat myasthenia gravis. Clinical studies show promising response rates, supporting its status as an effective emergent therapy for this condition.  

(Note: The summary strictly adheres to two sentences focusing only on clinically significant aspects related to the query.) 

The provided answer correctly identifies efgartigimod’s pharmacological action—blocking FcRn to reduce harmful anti-AChR IgGs—and highlights evidence from multiple sources showing favorable patient responses, which confirms it has proven clinical benefit in managing myasthenia gravis. This establishes both mechanistic rationale and real-world effectiveness based on documented outcomes in treated populations.
